understanding.goal_understanding: The game goal was to navigate a grid-based puzzle, matching the shape, color, and rotation of my carried piece to the requirements of goal cells in order to activate and complete them. Each level contained one or more goals, and I had to step onto each matching goal cell to complete it. Completing all goals in a level advanced to the next level, with the ultimate objective of winning all levels. I had to manage my stamina and avoid losing all lives.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The game mechanics involve moving a piece on a grid with various modifier tiles. Each move costs 1 stamina, and running out of stamina costs a life and resets stamina to max. Losing all lives ends the game. Walls block movement, indicated by 'blocked_directions'. Stepping on modifier tiles changes the piece's attributes: shape modifier cycles shape (0–5), color modifier cycles color (orange→blue→green→red→orange), and rotation modifier rotates 90° clockwise (0°→90°→180°→270°→0°). Goal cells activate only when the piece's shape, color, and rotation all match the goal's requirements simultaneously. Stepping onto an activated goal completes it. The grid uses ASCII representations for tiles (walls, floors, modifiers, stamina refills). I had to plan routes carefully, avoiding blocked directions, using modifiers strategically to match goals, and conserving stamina while completing all goals in each level.
